# Docker Hytale Server Container — Lightweight Self-Hosted Hytale Game Server

Repository: https://github.com/deinfreu/hytale-server-container
Documentation: https://hytale-server-container.com
Docker Hub: https://hub.docker.com/r/deinfreu/hytale-server
License: Apache-2.0 (code), GPL-3.0 (container)

## Description

The Hytale Server Container is a lightweight, production-ready Docker image for self-hosting Hytale dedicated game servers. Built from Alpine Linux with the Liberica JDK, it ships at just 61.7MB — making it one of the smallest available options. It supports both x86_64 and ARM64 architectures, runs as a non-root user by default for security, and includes the `hytale-downloader` CLI tool for managing server binaries and performing in-game updates without rebuilding the container.

The project provides comprehensive documentation covering installation via Docker CLI or Docker Compose, platform-specific guides for Debian 13, Raspberry Pi (ARM64), TrueNAS Scale, Unraid, and Windows WSL2, plus a debug diagnostic suite that audits network configurations and security settings during startup. It is actively maintained by 10+ contributors and designed for stability, performance, and ease of use.

## Core Capabilities

- **Automated binary management:** The `hytale-downloader` CLI tool downloads, installs, and updates Hytale server binaries from within the container terminal — no image rebuild required.
- **Tiny footprint:** Alpine-based images starting at 61.7MB with Liberica JDK for optimal performance.
- **Multi-architecture support:** Native x86_64 builds and ARM64 support via QEMU emulation (Raspberry Pi).
- **Non-root security:** Container runs as unprivileged user `1000:1000` by default, following Docker best practices.
- **Diagnostic suite:** Built-in debug mode (`DEBUG=TRUE`) that automatically audits network configurations, security settings, and permissions on startup.
- **Persistent storage:** Full volume support for world data, configs, logs, and backups — survives container restarts and redeploys.
- **In-game updates:** Run `hytale-downloader` inside the terminal to update server binaries while preserving all user data.
- **Configurable runtime:** Environment variables control server port, IP, game mode, player limits, compression, world selection, timezone, caching, backup frequency, and more.

## Architecture & Images

The project provides three Dockerfile variants:

| Image Tag | Base OS | JDK | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|-----|---------|
| `latest` (Ubuntu) | Ubuntu | OpenJDK | Full-featured development image |
| `latest-alpine` | Alpine Linux | OpenJDK | Minimal footprint production image |
| `latest-alpine-liberica` | Alpine Linux | Liberica JDK | Smallest image (~61.7MB), recommended for production |

All images share the same entrypoint script (`entrypoint.sh`) which orchestrates dependency installation, binary downloading, authentication, and server startup.

## Configuration

Server behavior is controlled entirely through environment variables — no manual file editing required:

### Core Settings
- `TZ` — Timezone for logs (default: `UTC`)
- `DEBUG` — Enable diagnostic audits (`FALSE` by default)
- `PROD` — Run production readiness checks (`FALSE` by default)
- `SERVER_PORT` — UDP port for game traffic (`5520`)
- `SERVER_IP` — Bind address (`0.0.0.0`)

### Hytale Server Options (config.json injection)
- `HYTALE_SERVER_NAME` — Display name in server browser
- `HYTALE_MOTD` — Message of the Day
- `HYTALE_PASSWORD` — Private server password
- `HYTALE_MAX_PLAYERS` — Concurrent player limit (`100`)
- `HYTALE_MAX_VIEW_RADIUS` — Chunk render distance (`32`)
- `HYTALE_COMPRESSION` — Network compression toggle (`false`)
- `HYTALE_WORLD` — World folder to load (`default`)
- `HYTALE_GAMEMODE` — Default game mode (`Adventure`)

### Advanced Options
- `HYTALE_CACHE` — Enable Ahead-Of-Time caching (`FALSE`)
- `HYTALE_BACKUP` — Auto-backup on startup (`FALSE`)
- `HYTALE_BACKUP_DIR` — Backup storage directory (`./backups`)
- `HYTALE_BACKUP_FREQUENCY` — Backup interval in minutes
- `HYTALE_BACKUP_MAX_COUNT` — Maximum retained backups
- `JAVA_ARGS` — Custom JVM flags

## Storage & Volumes

The container uses `/home/container` as its working directory. All persistent data should be mounted here:

| Container Path | Purpose |
|----------------|---------|
| `/home/container` | World files, configs, logs, and server data |
| `/etc/machine-id` (read-only) | Persistent host machine identity for server authorization |

Machine-ID persistence is critical — without it, the server regenerates its identity on each restart, requiring re-authentication. On systems where `/etc/machine-id` does not persist reliably (WSL2, Unraid, Raspberry Pi), create a local `machine-id` file and mount it read-only.

## Authentication Flow

Hytale requires account authentication during initial setup:

1. Start the container without `-d` flag to access the terminal.
2. The entrypoint script displays a verification URL with an embedded code.
3. Open the link in a browser, log into your Hytale account.
4. After authorization, server binaries are downloaded and the server starts automatically.

A second authentication step occurs after the server boots — authorize the server instance itself using the same flow. Once complete, the server is ready for player connections.

## CLI Tool: hytale-downloader

The container includes a built-in CLI tool accessible via `docker exec`:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `hytale-downloader` | Download latest release and install binaries |
| `hytale-downloader -print-version` | Show current Hytale game version |
| `hytale-downloader -version` | Show hytale-downloader tool version |
| `hytale-downloader -check-update` | Check for CLI updates |
| `hytale-downloader -patchline pre-release` | Download from the pre-release channel |
| `hytale-downloader -skip-update-check` | Skip automatic update check on startup |

## Documentation Site Structure

The documentation is a Jekyll site using the Just the Docs theme, deployed at https://hytale-server-container.com:

- **Home** — Overview, features, quick start links
- **Installation** — Requirements, Docker CLI/Compose setup, running the server, support
- **Guides** — Platform-specific tutorials (Debian 13, Raspberry Pi, TrueNAS Scale, Unraid, Windows WSL2)
- **Documentation** — Docker configuration reference, Hytale settings, development guide
- **FAQ** — Common issues: re-authentication loops, permission errors, updates, in-game commands

## Use Cases

- Self-hosting Hytale dedicated servers for personal or community use
- Mod development and testing environments
- Persistent multiplayer worlds with automated backups
- CI/CD pipeline integration via Docker Compose
- Edge deployment on ARM64 hardware (Raspberry Pi)
- Homelab server deployments on TrueNAS Scale or Unraid

## Contributing

The project welcomes contributions under GPL-3.0. See the GitHub repository for coding standards and pull request guidelines. Join the Discord community for support discussions and feature requests.
